market_overview: Bursa Malaysia remains selective rather than broadly risk-on. The higher-quality opportunity set is concentrated in three connected themes: data-centre power demand, AI infrastructure build-out, and defensive cash-flow compounders. The tactical bias is to avoid chasing illiquid spikes and focus on names where catalysts are paired with balance-sheet resilience, visible order books, or regulated earnings.

breadth.base_case: Accumulation favours names with real earnings translation from data-centre capex and grid expansion.
breadth.bear_case: A fast rotation out of AI and construction proxies would pressure small caps first; keep position sizing tight below RM0.50 names.
breadth.bull_case: Fresh data-centre awards, grid capex clarity, or foreign fund inflow could extend leadership in TNB, Gamuda, SNS and adjacent contractors.

portfolio_stance.summary: Keep a core allocation in liquid leaders, reserve tactical risk for confirmed breakouts, and leave cash for pullbacks after headline-driven moves.

top_picks.name: TENAGA
top_picks.confidence_basis: Regulated grid monopoly plus data-centre electricity demand
top_picks.analysis: TNB is the cleanest large-cap proxy for Malaysia's data-centre electricity requirement. The thesis is less about one project and more about recurring grid connection, transmission capex and regulated returns.

levels.risk_line: Weakness below RM13.80 changes the near-term structure

metrics.valuation_view: Premium defensible if RP4 returns and grid capex visibility hold

deep_dive.thesis: TNB is evolving from a conventional utility into the grid backbone for Malaysia's digital infrastructure. Data-centre growth creates a multi-year need for generation adequacy, transmission upgrades and connection reliability.
catalysts: Data-centre power reservation and connection demand
National Energy Transition Roadmap grid investment
risks: Capex timing may lag market expectations

top_picks.name: GAMUDA
top_picks.confidence_basis: Order-book depth and regional execution record
top_picks.analysis: Gamuda remains the highest-quality construction exposure because earnings are supported by a broad order book, overseas execution and potential domestic infrastructure optionality.

levels.risk_line: Break below RM4.05 implies deeper consolidation

metrics.valuation_view: Quality premium supported by execution and overseas mix

deep_dive.thesis: Gamuda is a core infrastructure compounder rather than a pure headline trade. Its regional engineering book cushions delays in local mega-project awards while data-centre and transport infrastructure provide upside optionality.
catalysts: Domestic rail and transport project flow

top_picks.name: SNS
top_picks.confidence_basis: AI infrastructure optionality with small-cap volatility
top_picks.analysis: SNS offers direct thematic exposure to enterprise hardware, AI servers and GPU-related services. The opportunity is meaningful, but position sizing should reflect small-cap liquidity and execution risk.

levels.trigger: Close above RM0.385 on rising volume
levels.risk_line: Below RM0.315 invalidates the breakout structure

deep_dive.thesis: The market is beginning to price SNS as an AI infrastructure participant instead of a pure reseller. Upside depends on converting partnerships and MoUs into recurring server, cloud or GPUaaS economics.
risks: MoU does not translate into material revenue

top_picks.name: GBGAQRS
top_picks.confidence_basis: Data-centre infrastructure proxy but liquidity constrained
top_picks.analysis: GBGAQRS gives leveraged exposure to the data-centre construction theme. The asymmetry is attractive only if volume confirms institutional participation and project scope becomes more concrete.

levels.risk_line: Below RM0.155 removes the tactical setup

metrics.valuation_view: Discount can close only with visible awards

deep_dive.thesis: AQRS is a higher-risk way to express the same AI infrastructure theme. It needs project conversion and clean execution to move from narrative discount to earnings-backed rerating.
what_to_watch: Net debt and operating cash flow

top_picks.name: INARI
top_picks.confidence_basis: Quality semiconductor pullback, but cyclical earnings risk remains
top_picks.analysis: Inari is a better-quality tech name for investors who want semiconductor exposure without chasing the smaller AI infrastructure trades. The setup needs patience because global tech rotation can still dominate local fundamentals.

metrics.valuation_view: Attractive only if RF and OSAT recovery become visible

deep_dive.thesis: Inari is a recovery-and-quality trade. The cleaner setup is to accumulate into weakness after confirmation that smartphone RF demand and outsourced semiconductor activity are stabilising.
catalysts: Cleaner capital allocation after non-core exits

top_picks.name: ITMAX
top_picks.analysis: ITMAX is less exposed to semiconductor cyclicality and more tied to recurring smart-city infrastructure, surveillance and analytics contracts. It belongs on the watchlist for pullbacks.

levels.risk_line: Loss of RM3.20 would reset the setup

metrics.valuation_view: Premium justified only by contract wins and recurring revenue

deep_dive.thesis: ITMAX adds a defensive-growth technology sleeve. Smart-city contracts can create more durable revenue than one-off hardware sales, but valuation discipline matters.

top_picks.name: SLVEST
top_picks.analysis: Solarvest provides renewable-energy exposure that complements the grid and data-centre theme. It is more sensitive to project timing and margin execution than TNB.

levels.risk_line: Below RM1.34 weakens the growth setup

metrics.balance_sheet: Project-driven; monitor leverage and cash cycle
metrics.valuation_view: Depends on sustained EPCC and recurring asset ownership mix

deep_dive.thesis: Solarvest is a medium-beta way to participate in Malaysia's energy transition. Stronger upside comes if it shifts more earnings toward recurring asset ownership rather than pure project delivery.

top_picks.name: IHH
top_picks.analysis: IHH is not the highest-upside idea, but it improves portfolio balance if the AI and construction trades become crowded. Healthcare earnings are less tied to local market risk appetite.

metrics.valuation_view: Quality premium; less tactical upside than infrastructure names

deep_dive.thesis: IHH is a defensive anchor for a Shariah portfolio. It offers regional healthcare exposure and lowers dependence on local policy or construction cycles.
